selection_reasons.repair-falsify-5x3-efficient: pooled champion across 19 fresh panels: 106/246 exact; 41.5% weakest family
selection_reasons.repair-falsify-9x1-efficient: current panel winner retained unchanged for replication

source: mechanical anchors plus iteration 22 Sol director

strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: Retain the pooled champion unchanged. Its parallel falsifying repair panel remains the principal replicated control for answer-generating review.
strategies.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Retain the operationally equivalent nine-bank one-review mechanism as the close allocation and call-efficiency probe. It tests whether spending calls on independent candidate coverage rather than extra reviewers can overcome the selector gap on a fresh panel.
strategies.name: One-Review Nine-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Replicate the frozen pipeline unchanged because regeneration continues to add correct candidates and its pooled helpful-to-harmful intervention balance remains strongly positive; the test is whether the terminal integrator can close more of the expanded-oracle gap.
strategies.name: Blind Regeneration then Falsifying Integration

strategies.hypothesis: Sequential falsification may improve candidate integration by forcing each reviewer to attack one explicit predecessor proposal, providing an orthogonal test against independent review plurality without increasing the champion's call count.
strategies.name: Sequential Five-Bank Cross-Examination
